step_by_step_thinking:
To calculate Net Filtration Pressure (NFP), we use the formula NFP = (PGC - pGC) - PBC. Given the provided values, let's perform this calculation step by step to determine if there is enough information or if additional details might be needed.

Applying the given values into the equation:

\[ \text{NFP} = (\text{PGC} - \text{pGC}) - \text{PBC} \]
\[ \text{NFP} = (42\;mmHg - 12\;mmHg) - 16\;mmHg \]

Now performing the arithmetic calculations will give us our result directly from these figures.

My answer is A: 34 mm Hg. The problem provides all necessary parameters for calculating the net filtration pressure accurately without any ambiguity about missing protein content as stated in the scenario. Applying the given values into the equation:

\[ \text{NFP} = (\text{PGC} - \text{pGC}) - \text{PBC} \]
\[ \text{NFP} = (42\;mmHg - 12\;mmHg) - 16\;mmHg \]

Now performing the arithmetic calculations will give us our result directly from these figures.
